label: PASS
rationale: MBRControl was set Enable=True, Done=True, DoneOnReset={Power Cycle}. A PowerCycle is a listed reset event, so Done is set False; with MBREnabled still true, MBRDone must be 0.

label: FAIL
rationale: MBRControl DoneOnReset included Power Cycle and the trajectory performed PowerCycle, so Done should be set False. Since MBRDone is one only when MBREnabled is one and Done is True, returning MBRDone=1 is non-compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: MBRControl was set Enable=True and Done=True with an empty DoneOnReset set. The core text says an empty set means reset does not change Done, so after PowerCycle Done remains True and MBRDone=1 is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The trajectory set Done=True and DoneOnReset empty. Because an empty DoneOnReset set means no reset changes Done, PowerCycle should leave Done True; with MBREnabled true, MBRDone should be 1, not 0.

label: PASS
rationale: ProgrammaticResetEnable=True enables TPER_RESET. TPER_RESET is the Programmatic reset event, and DoneOnReset included Programmatic, so TPER_RESET sets MBRControl.Done False; MBRDone=0 is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: With ProgrammaticResetEnable=True, TPER_RESET is enabled and corresponds to the Programmatic reset type. Because DoneOnReset contained Programmatic, TPER_RESET should set Done False, making MBRDone=0 rather than 1.

label: PASS
rationale: MBRControl was set Enable=True and Done=False with DoneOnReset empty. PowerCycle does not change Done when the set is empty, and MBRDone is one only when Done is True, so MBRDone=0 is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: MBRControl Done was set False and DoneOnReset was empty, so PowerCycle should not change Done. Since MBRDone requires Done=True, returning MBRDone=1 is non-compliant.
